assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: The response mentions that jobs can't be live-migrated and that slices are affected, but doesn't specifically explain that TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step rather than through rolling upgrades

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: The response clearly states 'Even if only one slice needs maintenance, your entire multislice job stops due to tight coupling'

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: The response explains that the training job will be killed and terminated, indicating simultaneous interruption across all slices

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: While the response mentions 'Quota availability: Ensure your TPU quota can accommodate the restart', it doesn't explain that slices block until hardware is freed or the potential for extended downtime due to resource unavailability

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: The response includes 'Save checkpoint before maintenance window' as part of the proactive restart strategy

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: The response mentions 'ensure checkpoint recovery is bulletproof' and states that 'modern training frameworks handle checkpoint recovery well'

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: The response does not mention maintenance exclusions or the ability to defer maintenance events for training runs near completion

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: While the response mentions checking maintenance windows for monitoring purposes, it doesn't suggest using maintenance windows to schedule events during planned breaks in training
